title: Risk evaluation and management strategy involving patient follow-ups relating to a complement inhibitor
abstract: This invention provides, inter alia , a complement-inhibitor-based treatment plan coupled with a risk evaluation and management strategy ("REMS") and a safety support program ("SSP") for reinforcing the REMS. The REMS and SPP are implemented using one or more computer devices with software tools programmed to enforce conditions of the REMS and/or prompt follow-ups by registered nurses enrolled in the SSP. The software tool(s) determines whether a prescriber requesting the complement inhibitor has agreed to abide by the REMS, and can prompt a provider of the complement inhibitor to provide updated educational materials to the prescriber at predetermined times or intervals, to monitor the prescriber for compliance with the REMS, and/or to monitor patients for signs of adverse events. Using exemplary embodiments described herein, a risk of adverse events (especially, but not limited to, meningococcal infections) can be managed and an incidence of the adverse events can be reduced.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: receiving a request from a prescriber to distribute a complement inhibitor; verifying that the prescriber has agreed to the following conditions: (a) to vaccinate patients with a Neisseria meningococcal type B specific vaccine, and/or revaccinate patients according to a pre-determined schedule with a Neisseria meningococcal type B specific vaccine at predetermined times or intervals, (b) to provide the patients with educational materials regarding the complement inhibitor, and (c) that the prescriber has reviewed information relating to administering the complement inhibitor; accessing, using a computer with a processor programmed to perform the accessing, a database of certified prescribers; adding, using the processor, the prescriber to the database of certified prescribers, the adding performed only if the prescriber has agreed to conditions (a) - (c) in order to counsel the patient with regard to the risks associated with the complement inhibitor and is vaccinated or revaccinated to prevent a meningococcal infection, and that the prescriber is educated with regard to administering the complement inhibitor to the patient; receiving a requisition from the prescriber, the requisition requesting that the prescriber be provided with the complement inhibitor; inspecting, using the processor, the database of certified prescribers; and authorizing, using the processor, the prescriber to receive the complement inhibitor only if the prescriber is in the database of certified prescribers. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein an entry in the database associated with the prescriber is associated with one or more flags certifying that the prescriber has agreed to comply with conditions 

 (a) - (c); and the prescriber is only authorized to receive the complement inhibitor if the one or more flags indicate that the prescriber has agreed to comply with conditions (a) - (c). 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, further comprising prompting, after a predetermined period of time, a reminder to follow up with the prescriber as to whether the prescriber is aware of an adverse event associated with providing the complement inhibitor to the patient; and upon prompting, the computer comprises an interface and generates a reminder to send to the prescriber. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the adverse event is an infection caused by a meningococcal bacteria. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the adverse event is hemolysis. 6. The method of any of the preceding claims, further comprising prompting, after a predetermined period of time, a reminder to provide the prescriber existing or updated educational materials about the complement inhibitor; and an interface to send the reminder to the prescriber. 7. The method of any of the preceding claims, further comprising transmitting, after a predetermined period of time, a survey regarding the prescriber's compliance with one or more of conditions (a)-(c). 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising receiving a report from the prescriber that a patient was lost to follow up; identifying a new prescriber associated with the patient; and performing at least one of the following: verifying that the new prescriber is in the database of certified prescribers, or 

 verifying that the patient is no longer being treated with the complement inhibitor, and triggering a post-treatment reminder to follow up with the patient regarding the occurrence of any adverse events following discontinuation of the complement inhibitor. 9. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the patient has been diagnosed with a complement-associated disorder. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the patient has been diagnosed with paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria ("PNH"), atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome ("aHUS"), or Shiga-toxin-producing E. coli hemolytic uremic syndrome ("STEC-HUS"). 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the patient has been diagnosed with a complement- associated disorder selected from the group consisting of age-related macular degeneration, graft rejection, bone marrow rejection, kidney graft rejection, skin graft rejection, heart graft rejection, lung graft rejection, liver graft rejection, rheumatoid arthritis, a pulmonary condition, ischemia- reperfusion injury, atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome, thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura, paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria, dense deposit disease, age-related macular degeneration, spontaneous fetal loss, Pauci-immune vasculitis, epidermolysis bullosa, recurrent fetal loss, multiple sclerosis, traumatic brain injury, myasthenia gravis, cold agglutinin disease, dermatomyositis, Degos' disease, Graves' disease, Hashimoto's thyroiditis, type I diabetes, psoriasis, pemphigus, autoimmune hemolytic anemia, idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura, Goodpasture syndrome, multifocal motor neuropathy, neuromyelitis optica, antiphospholipid syndrome, sepsis, hemorrhagic fever, and catastrophic antiphospholipid syndrome. 12. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the complement inhibitor is eculizumab or an eculizumab variant. 13. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the Neisseria meningococcal type B specific vaccine is multicomponent meningococcal serogroup B vaccine (4CMenB) or 

 meningococcal group B vaccine (Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B recombinant lp2086 a05 protein variant antigen and Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B recombinant lp2086 bOl protein variant antigen). 14. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing instructions that, when executed by a processor of a computer, cause the processor to: receive verification that a prescriber requesting to administer a complement inhibitor has agreed to the following conditions: (a) to vaccinate and/or revaccinate patients receiving the complement inhibitor, at predetermined times or intervals, with one or more meningococcal vaccines, including a Neisseria meningococcal type B specific vaccine; (b) to provide patients with educational materials regarding the complement inhibitor, and (c) that the prescriber has reviewed information relating to administering the complement inhibitor; access a database of certified prescribers; add the prescriber to the database only if the prescriber has agreed to conditions (a) - (c) in order to counsel the patient with regard to the risks associated with the complement inhibitor and is vaccinated or revaccinated to prevent a meningococcal infection, and that the prescriber is educated with regard to administering the complement inhibitor to the patient; inspect the database of certified prescribers in response to a requisition from the prescriber requesting that the prescriber be provided with the complement inhibitor; and authorize the prescriber to prescribe the complement inhibitor only if the prescriber is in the database of certified prescribers. 15. The medium of claim 14, wherein: an entry in the database associated with the prescriber is associated with one or more flags certifying that the prescriber has agreed to comply with conditions (a) - (c); and 

 the prescriber is only authorized to receive the complement inhibitor if the one or more flags indicate that the prescriber has agreed to comply with conditions (a) - (c). 16. The medium of any one of claims 14-15, further storing instructions for prompting, after a predetermined period of time, a reminder to follow up with the prescriber as to whether the prescriber is aware of an adverse event associated with providing the complement inhibitor to a patient. 17. The medium of any one of claims 14-16, further storing instructions for prompting, after a predetermined period of time, a reminder to provide the prescriber existing or updated educational materials about the complement inhibitor. 18. The medium of any one of claims 14-17, further storing instructions for transmitting, after a predetermined period of time, a survey regarding the prescriber's compliance with one or more of conditions (a)-(c). 19. The medium of any one of claims 14-18, further storing instructions for: receiving a report from the prescriber that a patient was lost to follow up; and identifying a new prescriber associated with the patient; and performing at least one of the following: verifying that the new prescriber is in the database of certified prescribers, or verifying that the patient is no longer being treated with the complement inhibitor, and triggering a post-treatment reminder to follow up with the patient regarding the occurrence of any adverse events following discontinuation of the complement inhibitor. 20. The medium of any one of claims 14-19, wherein the complement inhibitor is eculizumab or an eculizumab variant. A method, comprising: 

 receiving a request from a patient enrolled in a treatment plan employing a complement inhibitor to be enrolled in a safety support program; adding, using a computer with a processor suitably programmed to perform the adding, the patient to a patient safety registry; and automatically prompting, using the processor, an authorized user to follow up with the patient to discuss the possibility of one or more adverse events associated with the complement inhibitor, the prompting instructing the authorized user to discuss: (a) symptoms of the adverse events, and (b) whether the patient has experienced an unreported adverse event, in order to counsel the patient with regard to the risks associated with the adverse event when being treated with the complement inhibitor, wherein the prompting is automatically performed at one or more predetermined times or intervals. 22. The method of claim 21, further comprising: accessing, using the processor, a database of registered nurses participating in the safety support program, wherein the authorized user is a registered nurse from the database. 23. The method of claims 21-22, wherein the adverse event is a meningococcal infection. 24. The method of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the prompting further instructs the authorized user to ensure that the patient possesses a copy of a client safety card. 25. The method of any one of claims 21-24, further comprising inspecting the patient registry to determine a most-recent date of vaccination against a meningococcal infection, including vaccination with a vaccine to Neisseria meningitidis serotype B; determining that the patient is due for revaccination; and automatically prompting the authorized user to send a revaccination reminder to the patient. 26. The method of any one of claims 21-25, further comprising: identifying that the patient has stopped use of the complement inhibitor; logging the stoppage in the patient safety registry; and automatically prompting an updated follow-up based on a risk of adverse conditions following cessation of the complement-inhibitor-based treatment plan. 27. The method of any one of claims 21-26, further comprising performing a follow up by a Nurse Case Manager following cessation of the complement-inhibitor-based treatment, wherein the risk of adverse conditions is hemolysis. 28. The method of any one of claims 21-27, wherein the complement inhibitor is eculizumab or an eculizumab variant. 29. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium holding instructions that, when executed by a processor of a computer, cause the processor to: receive a request from a patient enrolled in a complement-inhibitor-based treatment plan to be enrolled in a safety support program; add the patient to a patient safety registry; and automatically prompt an authorized user to follow up with the patient to discuss the possibility of one or more adverse events associated with the complement inhibitor, the prompting instructing the authorized user to discuss: (a) symptoms of the adverse events, and (b) whether the patient has experienced an unreported adverse event, in order to counsel the patient with regard to the risks associated with the adverse event when being treated with the complement inhibitor, wherein the prompting is automatically performed at one or more predetermined times or intervals. 30. The medium of claim 29, further storing instructions for accessing, using the processor, a database of registered nurses participating in the safety support program, wherein the authorized user is a registered nurse from the database. 31. The medium of any one of claim 29-30, wherein the adverse event is a meningococcal infection. 32. The medium of any one of claim 29-31, wherein the prompting further instructs the authorized user to ensure that the patient possesses a copy of a client safety card. 33. The medium of any one of claim 29-32, further storing instructions for inspecting the patient registry to determine a most-recent date of vaccination against meningococcal infections; determining that the patient is due for revaccination; and prompting the authorized user to send a revaccination reminder to the patient. 34. The medium of any one of claim 29-33, further storing instructions for identifying that the patient has stopped use of the complement inhibitor; recording the stoppage in the patient safety registry; and automatically prompting an updated follow-up based on a risk of adverse conditions following cessation of the complement-inhibitor-based treatment plan. 35. The medium of any one of claim 29-34, further storing instructions for performing a follow up by a Nurse Case Manager following cessation of the complement-inhibitor-based treatment, wherein the risk of adverse conditions is hemolysis. 36. The medium of any one of claim 29-35, wherein the complement inhibitor is eculizumab or an eculizumab variant. 37. A method of treating a patient in need of treatment with a complement inhibitor, or inhibiting formation of a terminal complement in a patient, comprising: administering an effective amount of the complement inhibitor to said patient who has been prescribed the complement inhibitor by a certified prescriber of the complement inhibitor; wherein a prescriber becomes a certified prescriber of the complement inhibitor by: first requesting the provider over a network, which is part of a system comprising a computer with a processor, to become authorized to prescribe the complement inhibitor; the provider then verifying that the prescriber has agreed to the following conditions: (a) to vaccinate and/or revaccinate patients receiving the the complement inhibitor, at predetermined times or intervals, with one or more meningococcal vaccines, including a Neisseria meningococcal type B specific vaccine; (b) to provide patients with educational materials regarding the complement inhibitor, and (c) that the prescriber has reviewed information provided by the provider relating to the complement inhibitor; once verified, adding, using the processor, said prescriber to the database as a certified prescriber in order to counsel the patient with regard to the risks associated with the complement inhibitor and is vaccinated or revaccinated to prevent a meningococcal infection, and that the prescriber is educated with regard to administering the complement inhibitor to the patient; and then using the processor to generate a prescription approval code and authorizing, using the processor, the prescriber to prescribe to the patient the complement inhibitor only if the prescriber is in the database of certified prescribers.
